task_completion.description: Open the video export dialog for the loaded CT series
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or menu related to exporting the series to a video format (e.g., 'Export Video', 'Save as Movie') is visible in the medical imaging software.

task_completion.description: Set the frame rate to 10-15 fps
task_completion.visual_evidence: A field labeled 'Frame rate', 'FPS', or similar in the export settings shows a value between 10 and 15.
The save dialog's location bar or folder hierarchy shows the current directory as '~/DICOM/exports' or '/home/ga/DICOM/exports'.

task_completion.description: Set the output filename to anatomy_scroll.avi
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'anatomy_scroll.avi' (or 'anatomy_scroll.mp4') is entered in the 'File name' or 'Save as' field of the export dialog.
The agent clicks the 'Save', 'Export', or 'OK' button to finalize the export, or a progress bar is visible indicating the video is being generated.

integrity.description: The agent used the medical imaging software's GUI to export the video
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the application's menus and export dialogs, rather than terminal commands creating the file.

integrity.description: The export dialog belongs to the actual medical imaging software
integrity.visual_evidence: The export settings and dialogs are part of the medical viewer interface, not a text editor or mock application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to create a fake video file
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'touch', 'echo', or 'ffmpeg' being used to bypass the GUI export process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exported video must be named either 'anatomy_scroll.avi' or 'anatomy_scroll.mp4' and saved in the directory '/home/ga/DICOM/exports'. The file must be a valid video file with a minimum size of 10240 bytes.
